executiveSummary.text: Smurfit Westrock (SW) is now back in sync with the canonical DYOR HQ sheet after the metadata repair pass. The current stance is REDUCE with conviction 50/100, using $42.06 as the latest stored price reference where available.

businessModel.text: Smurfit Westrock is exposed to packaging volumes, pricing and synergy delivery, with the near-term thesis tied to demand normalisation and cost execution.

recentCatalysts.text: Box demand, mix, cost savings and balance-sheet progress remain the main drivers.

thesisEvaluation.text: Base case: the core operating backdrop remains good enough to support the present reduce stance, but not strong enough to justify a looser risk view without better evidence. Bull case: execution and demand improve faster than expected. Bear case: operating or funding pressure worsens and compresses valuation further.

keyRisks.text: Industrial and consumer demand can weaken. Pricing can fall faster than costs. Integration or synergy delivery can lag. Leverage can amplify disappointment. Energy and raw-material costs can stay volatile.

risks.risk: Industrial and consumer demand can weaken.
Pricing can fall faster than costs.
Integration or synergy delivery can lag.
Energy and raw-material costs can stay volatile.

whoShouldOwn.text: Own: Investors comfortable with cyclical packaging demand and restructuring risk. Avoid: Investors who need low leverage and highly stable volumes.

recommendation.text: REDUCE - Conviction: 50/100. This rebuilt page is intentionally concise, but it is now internally coherent and aligned to the corrected ticker and company metadata.

entryExit.text: Keep position sizing consistent with a reduce stance until fuller company-specific analysis is refreshed.

sources.marketData: Live quote data sourced via the DYOR HQ proprietary market data workflow and internal canonical mapping.
